novel_features.name: Author archive
novel_features.description: Mirror a writer's entire body of work into local SQLite, full-text searchable offline.
novel_features.rationale: No single API call returns an author's complete catalog with content; this resolves the username, pages through every article, fetches each body, and stores it locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need a complete, queryable copy of one author's writing rather than the 10-item RSS window or one-article-at-a-time fetches.

novel_features.name: Corpus search
novel_features.description: Full-text and regex search across everything you have synced locally (authors, publications, tags).
novel_features.rationale: Medium's own search is server-side, shallow, and uncomposable; this searches your mirrored corpus offline with SQL/regex.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to find a half-remembered passage across all the writing you have archived, without burning API calls.

novel_features.name: Topic pulse
novel_features.description: One ranked digest of what is surging in a topic, merging hot/new/top feeds, latest posts, and top writers for a tag.
novel_features.rationale: Requires combining four separate endpoints and de-duplicating locally; no single API call produces a topic overview.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a fast read on where a topic is heading this week instead of polling four endpoints by hand.

novel_features.name: Who writes about
novel_features.description: Surface the authoritative voices on a topic, ranked by output and engagement, from top writers, recommended users, and your archived articles.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across top-writers, recommended-users, and archived article authorship that no endpoint exposes together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to decide who to follow or archive on a subject, ranked by real output rather than a flat follow list.

novel_features.name: Author compare
novel_features.description: Compare two writers on output cadence, topic mix, and engagement (claps and voters per article) from locally archived data.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating each author's archived articles and computing distributions a single API response never carries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to weigh two writers or publications before committing to follow or archive one.

novel_features.name: New-since digest
novel_features.description: A deduped, ranked 'what is new since last sync' across the authors, publications, and tags you have archived.
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-windowed aggregation over the local store; no API call answers 'what changed for everything I track'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as a personal what-did-I-miss feed across everything you have synced, computed offline.

narrative.headline: Read any Medium author, publication, or tag as a local, full-text-searchable corpus.
narrative.value_prop: medium-pp-cli mirrors authors, publications, and tags into a local SQLite store, so you can archive a writer's entire body of work, search across everything you have synced, and see what is surging in a topic — offline, in one command, agent-native.
narrative.auth_narrative: Reads use Medium's own public surfaces directly — its RSS feeds, the article page, and Medium's internal GraphQL endpoint — with no API key and no third-party service. Member-locked article bodies can be unlocked by supplying your own Medium session cookie (MEDIUM_SESSION or --cookie-file); read anonymously, locked posts return Medium's public preview.

quickstart.comment: health check; confirms config and base URL without needing a key
resolve a username to its internal user id
see what is surging in a topic in one call
mirror an author's full catalog into the local store

troubleshoots.fix: Medium changed its internal GraphQL endpoint; feed and read keep working. Update the source adapter under internal/source and open a PR against the library.

troubleshoots.symptom: read returns only a short preview (is_preview_only: true)
troubleshoots.fix: the article is member-locked and you are reading anonymously; supply your own Medium session cookie (MEDIUM_SESSION or --cookie-file) to unlock the full body

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to read, archive, search, or analyze Medium content — a specific author's catalog, a publication's articles, a tag's trending feed, or the full text of an article. It is the right tool when the task is research, monitoring, or building a local corpus of Medium writing.
anti_triggers: Do not use to publish or update posts on Medium; the official write API was closed to new integrations in 2025.
Do not use to bulk-collect or redistribute copyrighted Medium content; this is for personal reading and research of content you have access to.
Do not use for non-Medium blogs or RSS in general.

recipes.title: Archive an author and search their work offline
recipes.command: medium-pp-cli author-archive uxdesigncc --agent && medium-pp-cli corpus "accessibility" --data-source local --agent
recipes.explanation: Mirror a writer's catalog once, then search it with no further API calls.

recipes.title: Topic scan with trimmed output
recipes.explanation: Get a topic digest and keep only the fields an agent needs to rank.

recipes.title: Pull a paywalled article you can read as a subscriber
recipes.explanation: Fetch the full markdown body of an article by its id.

trigger_phrases: what is trending in the design tag on Medium
get the full text of this Medium article
who writes about UX on Medium
